mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals an accommodative monetary regime that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset valuations. This mechanism operates continuously when the policy rate is at the effective lower bound, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% indicates near-zero policy rate environment
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate above 8.5% signals labor market slack and economic weakness that constrains corporate earnings growth and equity valuations. When UNRATE exceeds this threshold, S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days are suppressed due to reduced earnings expectations and elevated recession risk.
regime_hint: elevated unemployment and labor market weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE > 8.5%, or the coefficient on UNRATE in a predictive regression is not significantly negative (t-stat < 1.96).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth rate decelerates below 2% year-over-year, signaling weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate revenue growth. This demand contraction produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity prices adjust to lower earnings forecasts.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 395472 million requires YoY comparison for growth rate
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS growth and 5-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while remaining below 95.0 index level signals supply-side weakness and capacity underutilization. This joint condition indicates structural economic slack that depresses equity valuations and produces negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods when INDPRO < 95.0 with MoM decline, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) move in opposite directions over a 3-month window, indicating a demand composition shift away from goods consumption toward housing investment. This reallocation signal produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as housing-sensitive equities outperform and construction activity accelerates.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month trend positive AND RSAFS 3-month trend negative
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 571.0 thousand units, RSAFS at 395472 million
regime_hint: demand reallocation from goods to housing in late-cycle expansion
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (HOUST trend × RSAFS trend) has Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 50,000 persons per month while unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, signaling a labor market inflection point. This joint deterioration produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors reprrice recession probability.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM growth < 50k AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in prior month
regime_hint: labor market deterioration and recession risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (PAYEMS deceleration AND UNRATE rise) shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 225 index level while federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is below 0.5%, creating a negative real interest rate regime. This monetary policy lag produces positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as equity investors seek inflation hedges and real asset valuations compress discount rates.
regime_hint: negative real rates and inflation-driven monetary accommodation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (CPIAUCSL level × FEDFUNDS level) shows Rank IC < 0.035 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) is below trend (estimated at 2.5% annualized) while housing starts (HOUST) exceed 600 thousand units annualized, indicating demand-side weakness offset by housing strength. This regime-specific pattern produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as housing and construction equities outperform a slowing broader economy.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.5% AND HOUST > 600k units
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13271.8 billion chained 2017 dollars, HOUST at 571.0 thousand units
regime_hint: below-trend growth with housing resilience in late-cycle slowdown
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (GDPC1 growth × HOUST level) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
